title: 𝐁𝐂𝐓 𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐨 𝐀𝐜𝐞𝐬 ‘𝐅𝐨𝐫𝐜𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐤𝐨𝐧 𝐖𝐢𝐭𝐡’ 𝐢𝐧 𝟏𝟏𝐭𝐡 𝐏𝐇 𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐧
content: 𝐁𝐂𝐓 𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐨 𝐀𝐜𝐞𝐬 ‘𝐅𝐨𝐫𝐜𝐞 𝐭𝐨 𝐑𝐞𝐜𝐤𝐨𝐧 𝐖𝐢𝐭𝐡’ 𝐢𝐧 𝟏𝟏𝐭𝐡 𝐏𝐇 𝐑𝐨𝐛𝐨𝐭𝐡𝐨𝐧

title: Congratulations! BCT Robo Aces
content:
With a remarkable haul of eleven major awards, including the coveted overall championship, Brokenshire College TorilÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs (BCT) team of student roboticistsÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂknown as the Robo AcesÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂrose above fierce competition to secure their place among the best robotics teams in the country during the 11th Philippine Robothon, held in Iloilo City last Saturday, February 7.
Competing against top-performing schools from various regions nationwide, the Robo Aces, consisting of elementary, junior high school, and senior high school students, showcased exceptional skill, innovation, and teamwork across multiple robotics categories.
The teamÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs victory reflects months of rigorous preparation, problem-solving under pressure, and a shared commitment to excellence.
This yearÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs haul further broadens BCTÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs growing collection of awards earned from local and international competitions since the school began participating in robotics events in 2018.
Robotics education has long been a flagship feature of the schoolÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs curriculum, equipping students with future-ready skills in programming, engineering design, critical thinking, and collaborative problem-solving. 
Through a strong emphasis on hands-on learning, innovation-driven instruction, and exposure to competitive platforms, the program empowers learners to apply classroom concepts to real-world challenges, reinforcing the schoolÃÂÃÂ¢ÃÂÃÂÃÂÃÂs commitment to delivering quality, relevant, and globally competitive education.
